questions.text: Which model has seven layers including Application and Transport?
questions.explanation: The OSI reference model defines seven layers.

questions.text: On the Network+ exam, which reference model includes Session, Presentation, and Application layers?
questions.explanation: The OSI reference model defines seven layers.

questions.text: Which protocol provides connectionless delivery at Layer 4?
questions.explanation: UDP has no handshake or guaranteed delivery.

questions.text: Which IPv6 address is the loopback address?
questions.explanation: IPv6 loopback is ::1.

questions.text: Which subnet mask creates four equal subnets from a /24?
questions.explanation: /26 yields four subnets with 62 usable hosts each in a classful-style split.

questions.text: Which port does DNS use for queries primarily?
questions.explanation: DNS typically uses UDP/TCP port 53.

questions.text: Which wireless standard introduced WPA3?
questions.explanation: WPA3 strengthens Wi-Fi authentication and encryption.

questions.text: Which device operates at Layer 3 and routes between networks?
questions.explanation: Routers forward based on IP routing tables.

questions.text: Which cable type is immune to EMI for long campus runs?
questions.explanation: Fiber uses light, avoiding electrical interference.

questions.text: Which QoS mechanism marks packets for priority treatment?
questions.explanation: Differentiated Services Code Point marks traffic classes.

questions.text: Which topology has a single cable backbone with terminators?

options.text: Star over switch is not bus

questions.explanation: Classic bus topology uses coax with end terminators.

questions.text: Which IPv4 address is limited broadcast?
questions.explanation: 255.255.255.255 is local broadcast.

questions.text: Which protocol maps port numbers to services?
questions.explanation: IANA assigns well-known ports for services.

questions.text: Which encapsulation order is correct for TCP/IP stack?
questions.explanation: PDU names change per layer down the stack.

questions.text: Which IPv6 feature eliminates NAT need in pure deployments?
questions.explanation: 128-bit addresses reduce NAT dependency.

questions.text: Which network type spans a city between campuses?

options.text: SAN storage network not geographic MAN

questions.explanation: Metropolitan Area Networks cover cities.

questions.text: Which ICMP message supports ping?
questions.explanation: Ping uses ICMP echo messages.

questions.text: (Network+ pool) Supplemental concepts item 1: which best practice improves reliability?

options.text: Follow change control and monitor metrics

questions.explanation: Operational discipline reduces outages and misconfigurations.

questions.text: (Network+ pool) Supplemental concepts item 2: which best practice improves reliability?

options.text: Follow change control and monitor metrics

questions.explanation: Operational discipline reduces outages and misconfigurations.

questions.text: Which TWO are private IPv4 ranges? (Select TWO.)
questions.explanation: 192.168/16 and 172.16/12 are RFC 1918 private space.

questions.text: Which routing protocol is distance-vector and uses hop count?
questions.explanation: RIP selects paths by hop count with limits.

questions.text: Which VLAN ID is reserved on Cisco-style switches?
questions.explanation: VLAN 1 is default; vendors reserve certain IDs.

questions.text: Which PoE standard supports up to 30W per port (802.3at)?
questions.explanation: 802.3at (PoE+) delivers higher wattage.

questions.text: Which DNS server type holds authoritative zone records?
questions.explanation: Authoritative servers store zone data.

questions.text: Which WAN technology uses virtual circuits over packet switching?
questions.explanation: Many WANs use label-switched or VC technologies.

questions.text: Which firewall rule direction blocks inbound by default in hardened design?

options.text: Implicit deny inbound after explicit permits

questions.explanation: Default deny inbound is a security baseline.

questions.text: Which 802.11 mode bonds channels for higher throughput?
questions.explanation: Wider channels increase Wi-Fi data rates.

questions.text: Which device terminates many SSL VPN sessions?

options.text: VPN concentrator or firewall with VPN

questions.explanation: VPN gateways handle encrypted remote access.

questions.text: Which IPv6 configuration method uses routers to advertise prefixes?
questions.explanation: Router Advertisements enable SLAAC addressing.

questions.text: Which cable tester result indicates split pair wiring fault?

options.text: Wire map shows pins crossed between pairs

questions.explanation: Split pairs cause crosstalk and errors.

questions.text: Which SD-WAN benefit optimizes multi-link use?
questions.explanation: SD-WAN steers traffic across MPLS/broadband/LTE.

questions.text: Which switch feature prevents loops while allowing redundancy?
questions.explanation: STP blocks redundant paths to avoid loops.

questions.text: Which authentication protocol is used with WPA2-Enterprise?
questions.explanation: 802.1X controls port access before Wi-Fi data.

questions.text: Which IPv4 CIDR block has 14 usable host addresses?
questions.explanation: /28 leaves 4 host bits = 16 addresses, 14 usable.

questions.text: Which load balancer mode distributes based on source IP hash?

options.text: Round robin DNS only always layer 7

questions.explanation: Persistence sticks sessions to the same backend.

questions.text: Which transceiver form factor is hot-swappable on switches?
questions.explanation: SFP modules plug into switch slots for fiber/copper.

questions.text: Which TWO are required for a functioning DHCP IPv4 lease? (Select TWO.)
questions.explanation: DHCP scopes need addresses, mask, and usually gateway/DNS options.

questions.text: Which documentation shows device interconnections and ports?
questions.explanation: Diagrams map topology for operations.

questions.text: Which SNMP version adds encryption and authentication?
questions.explanation: SNMPv3 supports authPriv security models.

questions.text: Which backup type copies only changed blocks since last full?
questions.explanation: Incrementals store changes since last backup of any type.

questions.text: Which KPI measures time between failures?
questions.explanation: Mean Time Between Failures indicates reliability.

questions.text: Which change management step validates rollback?
questions.explanation: Rollback plans reduce outage risk.

questions.text: Which log source shows authentication failures on Linux?
questions.explanation: Auth logs capture SSH and sudo events.

questions.text: Which monitoring alert indicates interface saturation?

options.text: Sustained high utilization/errors on SNMP ifInOctets

questions.explanation: Interface counters reveal congestion and errors.

questions.text: Which wireless survey tool heatmaps signal strength?

options.text: Site survey software with AP on a map

questions.explanation: Surveys visualize RSSI and interference.

questions.text: Which SLA metric defines allowed yearly downtime?
questions.explanation: Availability SLAs express uptime targets.

questions.text: Which configuration management stores device configs centrally?

options.text: Local only on each device unversioned

questions.explanation: Versioned configs enable audit and rollback.

questions.text: Which patch window practice reduces user impact?
questions.explanation: Scheduled windows coordinate downtime.

questions.text: Which DNS monitoring detects hijacking or stale records?

options.text: Synthetic queries and DNSSEC validation where used

questions.explanation: Proactive DNS checks catch poisoned or stale answers.

questions.text: Which cable management aids airflow in racks?
questions.explanation: Proper dressing improves cooling and troubleshooting.

questions.text: Which wireless controller function pushes SSID configs to APs?
questions.explanation: Controllers provision lightweight APs at scale.

questions.text: Which incident severity involves total site outage?
questions.explanation: P1 incidents are business-critical outages.

questions.text: Which tool captures packets for protocol analysis?
questions.explanation: Packet captures decode conversations on the wire.

questions.text: Which TWO are network hardening steps? (Select TWO.)
questions.explanation: Disable unused services, ACLs, and secure management reduce attack surface.

questions.text: Which attack floods a target with traffic to deny service?
questions.explanation: Volume-based attacks overwhelm resources.

questions.text: Which port security feature limits MAC addresses on a switch port?
questions.explanation: Port security restricts learned MAC count.

questions.text: Which VPN protocol runs on SSL/TLS port 443 often?
questions.explanation: SSL VPNs traverse firewalls using HTTPS port.

questions.text: Which wireless attack uses rogue AP with same SSID?
questions.explanation: Evil twins trick clients into malicious APs.

questions.text: Which NAC solution checks endpoint health before network access?
questions.explanation: NAC validates compliance before admission.

questions.text: Which encryption protects Wi-Fi payloads in WPA2-PSK?
questions.explanation: WPA2 personal uses AES with CCMP.

questions.text: Which firewall type inspects application layer data?
questions.explanation: NGFWs include app-aware policies.

questions.text: Which certificate problem breaks HTTPS trust?
questions.explanation: Browsers warn on bad cert chains or expiry.

questions.text: Which segmentation limits east-west malware spread?
questions.explanation: Segmentation contains lateral movement.

questions.text: Which remote access method avoids exposing RDP directly?

options.text: VPN then RDP over private network

questions.explanation: VPN reduces exposure of management ports.

questions.text: Which attack captures DHCP offers to become default gateway?
questions.explanation: Rogue DHCP can redirect traffic via bad gateway.

questions.text: Which HIDS tool monitors host logs and integrity?
questions.explanation: HIDS agents detect file and log anomalies.

questions.text: Which wireless mitigation reduces rogue AP risk?

options.text: WIPS scanning and ACL on wired uplinks

questions.explanation: Wireless IPS detects unauthorized APs.

questions.text: Which authentication factor is a hardware token?
questions.explanation: Tokens are possession factors.

questions.text: Which protocol secures SNMP monitoring?
questions.explanation: SNMPv3 encrypts and authenticates messages.

questions.text: Which email security record helps prevent spoofing?
questions.explanation: Email auth records reduce forged sender mail.

questions.text: Which TWO mitigate VLAN hopping? (Select TWO.)

options.text: Enable native VLAN tagging best practices
Use default VLAN 1 for all user traffic

questions.explanation: Secure trunk/access port settings and VLAN pruning reduce hopping.

questions.text: High latency to a remote site over VPN. First check?

options.text: Measure path with traceroute/MTR and check link utilization

questions.explanation: Path and congestion analysis isolates WAN issues.

questions.text: Users get wrong IP subnet after office move. Cause?

options.text: Mis-scoped DHCP or relay pointing to old server

questions.explanation: DHCP scope/relay errors assign wrong networks.

questions.text: Intermittent CRC errors on one switch port. Fix?

options.text: Replace patch cable and inspect SFP/transceiver

questions.explanation: Bad cables or optics cause CRC/FCS errors.

questions.text: Wi-Fi clients connect but captive portal never appears. Check?

options.text: DNS and firewall rules for walled garden URLs

questions.explanation: Portals require DNS/HTTP allow lists to redirect.

questions.text: Single host cannot reach internet but peers can. Next?

options.text: Compare gateway, DNS, and ACL on host vs working peer

questions.explanation: Host-specific config or ACL differs from working stations.

questions.text: Duplicate IP warnings on LAN. Resolution?

options.text: Identify conflicting static/DHCP leases and reconcile

questions.explanation: Duplicate addresses break connectivity for both hosts.

questions.text: Slow file share on gigabit link. Verify?
questions.explanation: Half-duplex or 100 Mbps link reduces throughput.

questions.text: BGP session down after firewall change. Likely?
questions.explanation: BGP uses TCP port 179 between routers.

questions.text: VoIP choppy on congested link. Mitigate?

options.text: Implement QoS marking/queuing for voice VLAN

questions.explanation: Priority queuing protects real-time traffic.

questions.text: Remote office tunnel up but no routes to HQ subnets. Fix?
questions.explanation: Missing routes prevent cross-site reachability.

questions.text: SSL cert warning on internal site only. Cause?

options.text: Internal CA not trusted on client or cert name mismatch
External DNS root issue only always

questions.explanation: Trust store or SAN mismatch causes local HTTPS warnings.

questions.text: Multicast video fails across subnets. Need?

options.text: IGMP snooping and PIM routing configured
Flat single VLAN only always wrong for large net

questions.explanation: Multicast routing controls group membership across VLANs.

questions.text: Fiber link flaps every few minutes. Inspect?

options.text: Dirty connectors, bend radius, and optical power levels

questions.explanation: Physical layer issues cause intermittent fiber links.

questions.text: After lightning, switch stack partial failure. Step?

options.text: Check PoE budget, damaged ports, and redundant power

questions.explanation: Power surges damage ports/modules; verify hardware health.

questions.text: Captive portal auth works on Android not iOS. Check?

options.text: Private MAC/Wi-Fi privacy randomization breaking portal auth

questions.explanation: Random MAC features can break MAC-based portal policies.

questions.text: Traceroute stops at firewall. Interpretation?
questions.explanation: Filters hide hops; verify with allowed probes/end-to-end tests.

questions.text: (Network+ pool) Supplemental troubleshooting item 1: which best practice improves reliability?

options.text: Follow change control and monitor metrics

questions.explanation: Operational discipline reduces outages and misconfigurations.

questions.text: (Network+ pool) Supplemental troubleshooting item 2: which best practice improves reliability?

options.text: Follow change control and monitor metrics

questions.explanation: Operational discipline reduces outages and misconfigurations.

questions.text: Which TWO tools diagnose DNS issues? (Select TWO.)

options.text: Multimeter continuity on RJ-45 for app layer

questions.explanation: Query tools and zone checks validate DNS resolution.

acronyms.hint: Maps IP to MAC on LAN
Predecessor terminology for TLS on HTTPS

acronyms.expansion: Terminal Access Controller Access-Control System Plus
